title: US Naval Logistics: 20 Ships Maintain Middle East Blockade Operations as Ford Carrier Rotates
summary: Military Sealift Command and combat logistics force ships sustained fleet replenishment operations supporting the 20-ship US naval presence enforcing the Iranian port blockade.
contents:
## Logistics Operations Sustain Middle East Naval Presence

### Force Sustainment
With 20 US Navy ships maintaining operations in the Middle East — including the Abraham Lincoln and George H.W. Bush carrier strike groups — Military Sealift Command logistics vessels continue high-tempo replenishment operations. Fleet oilers and supply ships provide fuel, ammunition, and provisions necessary for sustained blockade enforcement.

### Blockade Logistics
US Central Command reported that 21 ships had complied with directions to turn around since the blockade's inception, indicating active interdiction operations requiring sustained naval presence and logistics support. The combat logistics force enables extended operations far from shore-based supply points.

### Ford Rotation Impact
The USS Gerald R. Ford's departure after 10 months at sea highlighted the logistical challenges of sustained deployment. Maintenance requirements for the Ford's advanced electromagnetic launch system and other next-generation systems necessitated the rotation, creating temporary capability adjustments.

### Prepositioned Assets
Maritime prepositioning ships remain available in the Diego Garcia and regional logistics hubs to support potential escalation scenarios, including ground force deployment options that US Central Command has briefed to President Trump.

### Sources
US Central Command updates, Gulf News, defense logistics reporting.
